comments.text: This problem is "claimed" to have been resolved by E. Li in [Li26b]. Note: The acknowledgement section states: The author acknowledges the use of OpenAI’s ChatGPT during the preparation of this manuscript. While it was used for ideation, formulation, proof exploration and refinement, narrowing the search space, programming, LaTeX formatting and other forms of orchestration, the author nonetheless takes full responsibility for the accuracy of the final contents of this paper.

Hmm. Well, they did claim it, without quotes. Normally that disclosure means collaboration and that the author has carefully checked. But Eric Li says in their bio they're "3rd year CS undergraduate", which is not a bad thing by itself, but they have claimed to solve multiple problems on this site in a row, with non-short papers. That does mean more scrutiny should be placed here than usual papers. Note we are not (and should not be) trying to discriminate or treat authors differently just because they use AI. But I do believe circumstances that invite scrutiny should be noticed and acted upon appropriately.
